title: populus deltoides subsp. wislizeni
intro: Populus deltoides subsp. wislizeni, also known as Black Cottonwood, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. Herbal preparations of Populus deltoides subsp. wislizeni include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
